description_long: This house features a symmetrical, classical design with a prominent central section flanked by two side wings. The central portion is elevated, accessed by a wide staircase, and features a covered porch supported by columns, giving it a stately appearance. Both side wings have gabled roofs and their own columned porches, creating a balanced and formal facade. The exterior is primarily light-colored, with dark roofing and green landscaping elements, including bushes along the rear. Large, arched windows are visible on the back, while the front showcases a grand entryway with steps and a double-height porch. The overall structure suggests a blend of Colonial and Neoclassical architectural influences, emphasizing symmetry and decorative columns.
description: A stately, symmetrical house with a central elevated section, columned porches, and gabled side wings. The facade features a grand staircase, large windows, and formal landscaping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grand staircase, central double-height porch with columns, and symmetrical side wings with gabled roofs.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the roof layout, central flat roof, and the spatial arrangement of the wings and entry steps.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying large arched windows, bushes along the back, and the symmetry of the side wings.
description_view_3: Opposite top-down view, emphasizing the rear rooflines and the back landscaping.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large arched windows at the rear
attribute_entryway_design: Grand central staircase leading to a double-height porch with classical columns and a covered entry
